snapshot.note: Snapshot, not canonical. Reflects code on commit 6217498 (post-ADR-260/261/262 merge). The dispatcher↔Reviewer key-drift fixes from commits e55d201, 85c9736, 6027459 are encoded in the invariants. Refer to SERVICE-MODEL.md and named ADRs for current canon.

operator.description: The principal — workspace owner. Authors substrate, approves proposals, addresses the Reviewer in chat.

cron.description: Render cron job (yarnnn-unified-scheduler) firing on schedule. Per ADR-260/261, individual recurrences may eventually move to per-recurrence Render Crons; current implementation polls.

system_agent.description: Renamed concept post-ADR-205. The orchestration chat surface (yarnnn.py) — addresses operator, narrates Reviewer actions per ADR-258 revised. Not persona-bearing (ADR-216).

reviewer.description: The persona-bearing judgment seat (one per workspace). Substrate at /workspace/review/. Invoked via unified invoke_reviewer(trigger, context) per ADR-256. Speaks in operator-authored persona voice.

specialist_sub_llm.description: Sub-LLM call dispatched by Reviewer via DispatchSpecialist primitive per ADR-261 D7. Six roles: researcher, analyst, writer, tracker, designer, reporting. Headless mode, curated tools, identical shape to Claude Code sub-agents.

anthropic_api.description: Claude Sonnet (capital decisions, specialists) and Haiku (chat, reflections, recurrences). Tracked in token_usage table per caller.

supabase_postgres.description: Primary datastore. Tables: workspace_files, workspace_file_versions, workspace_blobs, tasks (thin scheduling index), action_proposals, session_messages, chat_sessions, agent_runs, token_usage, activity_log, platform_connections.

supabase_storage.description: Binary storage. Output gateway uploads composed artifacts here; compose cache stored at {agent-outputs}/_compose-cache/{hash}.html.

output_gateway.description: yarnnn-render Docker service. POST /compose for HTML composition with content-addressed cache. POST /render for skill execution (chart, mermaid, image asset producers per ADR-130/177).

alpaca.description: Trading platform (ADR-187). Read: positions, account state. Write: order submission (when AUTONOMY permits). Outcome reconciliation: filled trades → _money_truth.md.

lemonsqueezy.description: Commerce platform (ADR-183). Read: products, subscribers, revenue. Write: products, discounts. Outcome reconciliation: order events → _money_truth.md.

platform_apis_readonly.description: Slack, Notion, GitHub — direct API clients (ADR-076). No platform_content table anymore (ADR-153); platform data flows live during task execution.

authored_substrate_write.label: ADR-209 write_revision()
authored_substrate_write.description: Every workspace_files mutation MUST route through authored_substrate.write_revision(). Three sequential DB operations: (1) upsert content-addressed blob in workspace_blobs by sha256, (2) insert row in workspace_file_versions with parent_version_id linking to previous head, (3) update workspace_files head_version_id pointer + content + metadata. authored_by attribution is required. Taxonomy: operator | yarnnn:<model> | agent:<slug> | specialist:<role> | reviewer:<identity> | system:<actor>.

token_ledger_write.label: token_usage ledger
token_ledger_write.description: Every Anthropic API call writes a row in token_usage with caller tag. Callers: reviewer (Sonnet, capital), reviewer-reflection (Haiku — note: ADR-256 unification kept caller names for telemetry continuity even though function-level reflection mode was unified), system-agent (Haiku/Sonnet, addressed turns), specialist:<role> (Sonnet, ADR-261 D7). Billing rate: 2x Anthropic API (ADR-171/172). Cache discount NOT passed through.

narrative_append.label: session_messages append (the Feed)
narrative_append.description: Per FOUNDATIONS Axiom 9, every invocation emits a narrative entry. Per ADR-258 revised, Reviewer actions emit per-action narration via reviewer_chat_surfacing.surface_reviewer_actions() — System Agent bubble per consequential successful action (FireInvocation/ProposeAction/WriteFile). Cognition tools stay as transient streaming-status; failed actions stay silent. Per ADR-259, the surface is the Feed (was 'chat'). session_messages.role: user | system_agent | reviewer | agent | external | system.

decisions_md_append.label: /workspace/review/decisions.md append
decisions_md_append.description: Every Reviewer verdict + every reactive observation appends to /workspace/review/decisions.md via reviewer_audit.append_decision() or append_recurrence_fire(). Substrate-as-truth: the file is the audit ledger, not the action_proposals.reviewer_reasoning column (which is a denormalization).

execution_event_record.label: execution_events ledger
execution_event_record.description: Per-dispatch row recording invocation outcome: success | failed | skipped, with error_reason taxonomy (balance_exhausted, spend_ceiling, capability_missing, no_active_session, etc.). Used by /work surface freshness signals and scheduler telemetry.

autonomy_gate.label: should_auto_execute_verdict()
autonomy_gate.description: Per-domain delegation + ceiling check from /workspace/context/_shared/_autonomy.yaml (ADR-254 file-format discipline). Levels: manual | bounded | autonomous, with ceiling_cents. Reviewer verdict 'approve' still routes to operator Queue if AUTONOMY doesn't permit; if permitted within ceiling, executes via handle_execute_proposal. ADR-229 D1: judgment-first ordering — Reviewer runs ALWAYS, autonomy filters whether verdict BINDS post-judgment.

balance_gate.label: Balance + daily spend ceiling
balance_gate.description: Two-step pre-LLM gate on judgment-mode dispatch: (1) workspace.balance_usd > 0 (hard stop at zero; topped up via signup grant $3, top-ups $10/$25/$50, Pro $19/mo = $20 balance reset), (2) daily spend ≤ DAILY_SPEND_CEILING_USD (ADR-250 Phase 3). Reactive triggers get silent skip on ceiling; addressed triggers get warn-but-proceed.

invariants.statement: All judgment-mode work routes through invoke_reviewer(trigger, context). There is no second entry point.
invariants.rationale: ADR-256 unified the previous four Reviewer functions (review_proposal, run_reflection, address_turn, heartbeat_turn) into one. Any code path that judges should call invoke_reviewer; the dispatcher↔Reviewer key-drift bug (commit e55d201) lived for days because nothing asserted this.

invariants.statement: Every workspace_files mutation produces exactly one workspace_file_versions row with authored_by + message attribution.
invariants.rationale: ADR-209 Authored Substrate. The revision chain is authoritative; workspace_files.head_version_id is a read-optimization pointer.
invariants.enforced_by: api/services/authored_substrate.py:244 (write_revision is the singular write path)

invariants.statement: Reviewer trigger taxonomy is exactly {addressed, reactive}. Sub-shape (proposal arrival vs recurrence fire) is detected from context-bag contents, not a third trigger value.
invariants.rationale: ADR-263 D2 collapsed the previous 'scheduled' value into 'reactive' — the Reviewer never sees 'scheduled'. Sub-shape detection: presence of recurrence_prompt/recurrence_slug = recurrence fire (Haiku); presence of proposal_row = proposal arrival (Sonnet).

invariants.statement: Mechanical-mode recurrences invoke NO Reviewer and NO LLM. Zero-cost path.
invariants.rationale: ADR-263 D5 + ADR-264 D2. Mechanical recurrences declare @primitive: <Name>(<args>) in their prompt; dispatcher parses and executes the handler deterministically. Balance gate is bypassed (mechanical work has no LLM cost).

invariants.statement: Reviewer cannot author into operator-authorship paths by default. Default lock set: MANDATE, AUTONOMY, IDENTITY, BRAND, CONVENTIONS, PRECEDENT, _operator_profile.md, _risk.md.
invariants.rationale: ADR-258 revised D3+D9. Reviewer's WriteFile is lock-gated via DEFAULT_REVIEWER_WRITE_LOCKS in workspace_paths.py. Operator's _locks.yaml can add or unlock specific paths. Independence enforced by attribution + locks, not by absence of WriteFile.

invariants.statement: AI Reviewer approval still routes through autonomy gate before executing. Verdict ≠ execution.
invariants.rationale: ADR-229 D1 judgment-first ordering. Reviewer judges; AUTONOMY decides binding. Without _autonomy.yaml permitting auto-execution within ceiling, approved proposals route to operator Queue.

invariants.statement: Reviewer never re-invokes itself reactively when source ∈ {reviewer_periodic, reviewer_addressed, reviewer_heartbeat}.
invariants.rationale: ADR-252 D5 + ADR-253 D5. Skips self-judgment loop. AUTONOMY gate still applies downstream.

invariants.statement: Recurrence declarations live in /workspace/_recurrences.yaml. No per-shape declaration files, no output_kind enum, no TASK_TYPES registry runtime dispatch.
invariants.rationale: ADR-261. The 'tasks' DB table is a thin scheduling index, fully reconstructable from filesystem state via materialize_scheduling_index().

scheduled.title: Scheduled trigger — cron-fired recurrence
scheduled.summary: Render cron tick → due-recurrence query → CAS claim → invocation_dispatcher.dispatch() → either Reviewer (judgment mode) or deterministic primitive (mechanical mode). Most autonomous work flows through here.

gates.predicate: context_domain has reviewable substrate (principles.md or _money_truth.md or _operator_profile.md)
should_auto_execute_verdict(): delegation level + ceiling_cents check from _autonomy.yaml
branches: denies or over ceiling → operator Queue

addressed.title: Addressed trigger — operator chat (the Feed)
addressed.summary: Operator types in the Feed (ADR-259, was 'chat') → POST /feed → session resolved, 10-message window built, surface content loaded → invoke_reviewer(trigger='addressed') → 12-round tool loop on Haiku → per-action narration to Feed → conversation.md compaction every 5 user messages.

mechanical.title: Mechanical dispatch — zero-LLM primitive execution
mechanical.summary: Cron-fired recurrence with mode=mechanical → directive parser extracts @primitive: <Name>(<args>) → HANDLERS registry lookup → capability gate against active platform_connections → handler executes deterministically → write_revision attributes the work as system:<actor>. Zero LLM cost, balance gate bypassed.

outcomes.title: Outcome reconciliation — money-truth substrate
outcomes.summary: Daily back-office recurrence → reconcile_user() → provider polymorphism (TradingOutcomeProvider, CommerceOutcomeProvider) calls Alpaca/LS APIs → ledger.fold_outcome_candidates() upserts per-domain /workspace/context/{domain}/_money_truth.md with idempotency frontmatter + rolling 7d/30d/90d windows → cross-domain _performance_summary.md regenerated → high-impact outcomes route to feedback.md (ADR-195 v2 Phase 5a).

specialist_subloop.title: Reviewer → DispatchSpecialist → sub-LLM loop (zoomed in)
specialist_subloop.description: Embedded inside any Reviewer loop (Flows 1, 2, 3). The Reviewer composes a brief (research goal, files-to-read, output contract) and dispatches a specialist sub-LLM. The specialist runs headless with curated tools (read-only + scoped scaffold), returns markdown summary, and the Reviewer reads the result on the next round.

curated tools"] --> sub_loop sub_loop["specialist tool loop:
ReadFile · SearchFiles ·
(scoped scaffold)"] --> output output["markdown output"] --> stringify stringify["_stringify_tool_result()
:385"] --> back_to_rev back_to_rev["tool_result → Reviewer round N+1"] sub_loop -.->|each LLM call| token([token_usage
caller=specialist:<role>])
